Like, what does that process look like for you to, like, facilitate the creation of that piece of content where you need to kind of get information out of somebody else?
Um, how do you usually run those?
Um, so...
So if I need to talk to someone who's more of an expert, if it's at Surfer or somewhere else, I imagine that they are quite busy.
So I come up with, I propose at least three different times to meet, and then they choose one, and I have my questions sent over to them in advance.
And we usually do a 30-minute call.
And of course, I have my note taker running in the background, but I do a 30-minute call.
I have them give me soundbites that they think that need to be included.
But other than that, I give them the chance to review the questions in advance, and then we talk about anything that's not mentioned.
And from that, I extrapolate the best soundbites.
I feel like I figure out what kind of content I can pull from it, whether it's going to be visuals, infographics, static posts, if it's going to be LinkedIn posts or what have you.
We haven't had the chance to do recording necessarily.
Like...
So...
Video recording, but it's usually just the written, the written stuff.
So I, yeah, I schedule time with them, give them the questions in advance, have my notes running in the background, ask them any doubts or clarifications, and ask them what needs to be included.

I also make sure if there's anything that needs to be completely exact, like a direct quote, then I keep that without editing or touching, just in case, because technical things, you know, I'm going to get those right.
